en:
The first important piece of work [218] in this new field awakened only the faintest echo, in spite of the fact that it offered a new and fundamental conception of the neuroses. Certain writers expressed their approbation, and then, on the next page, proceeded to explain their cases of hysteria in the good old way. It was much as if a man should subscribe fully to the idea of the earth's being spherical, and yet continue to represent it as flat. Freud's next publications [219] were practically unnoticed, although they contributed findings of immeasurable importance to the domain of psychiatry. When in 1900 he produced the first real psychological elucidation of the dream [220] (previously there had reigned over this territory a suitable nocturnal darkness), he was ridiculed; and when in the middle of the last decade he began to illumine the psychology of sexuality itself, [221] and at the same time the "Zürich school" decided to range itself on his side, a storm of abuse, sometimes of the coarsest kind, burst upon him, nor has it yet ceased to rage. At the last South-West German Congress of alienists in Baden-Baden, the adherents of the new psychology had the pleasure of hearing Hoche, University Professor of Psychiatry at Freiburg in Breisgau, describe the movement in a long and much-applauded address, as an outbreak of mental aberration among doctors . The old proverb: "Medicus medicum non decimat" was here quite put to shame. How carefully the question had been studied was shewn by the naïve remark of one of the most distinguished neurologists of Paris, which I myself heard at the International Congress in 1907: "It is true I have not read Freud's works (he did not happen to know any German!), but as for his theories, they are nothing but a mauvaise plaisanterie." Freud, dignified, masterly, once said to me, I first became clearly conscious of the value of my discoveries when they were met everywhere with resistance and anger; since that time I have judged the value of my work according to the degree of opposition provoked. It is against my sexual theory that the greatest indignation is felt, so it would seem therein lies my best work. Perhaps after all the real benefactors of mankind are its false teachers, for opposition to the false doctrine pushes men willy nilly into truth. Your truth-teller is a pernicious fellow, he drives men into error."

The reader must now calmly accept the idea that in this psychology he is dealing with something quite unique, if not indeed with some altogether irrational, sectarian, or occult wisdom; for what else could possibly provoke all the scientific authorities to turn away on the very threshold and utterly refuse to cross it?
zh:
第一項在這一新領域的重要著作[^218]僅喚起極微弱的迴響，儘管它對神經症提出了全新且根本性的概念。某些作者表達了贊同，然後在下一頁，卻繼續以舊有的良好方式來解釋他們的歇斯底里病例。這就好比一個人完全贊同地球是球形的觀念，卻仍舊將其描繪為平的。佛洛伊德接下來的幾部出版物[^219]幾乎無人注意，儘管它們為精神醫學領域貢獻了無可估量的重要發現。當他在1900年提出了第一個對夢的真正心理學闡釋[^220]（此前在這片領域上籠罩著恰當的夜間黑暗），他遭到了嘲笑；而當他在上一個十年的中期開始闡明性慾本身的心理學時，[^221]同時「蘇黎世學派」決定站在他這一邊，一場辱罵的風暴——有時是最粗鄙的那種——便向他襲來，至今仍未止息。在巴登-巴登最近一次德國西南精神科醫師大會上，新心理學的擁護者們有幸聽到弗萊堡大學精神醫學教授霍赫在一場長篇且備受喝采的演講中，將這場運動描述為醫生群體中心理失常的爆發。那句古老的諺語：「醫生不剝削醫生」在此完全蒙羞。對於這個問題的研究有多麼仔細，可由巴黎一位最傑出的神經學家天真的評論看出——這是我在1907年國際大會上親耳聽到的：「的確，我沒讀過佛洛伊德的著作（他碰巧不懂德文！），但至於他的理論，那隻不過是一場惡劣的玩笑。」佛洛伊德，帶著尊嚴與大師風範，曾對我說：「當我的發現到處都遇到抵抗與憤怒時，我才第一次清楚地意識到它們的價值；從那時起，我便根據所激起的反對程度來判斷我工作的價值。正是針對我的性理論，人們感到最大的憤慨，所以看來我最好的工作就在其中。畢竟，人類真正的恩人或許是那些虛假的教師，因為對虛假教義的反對，會迫使人們無論如何都進入真理。你那說真話的人是個有害的傢伙，他會把人驅入錯誤之中。」

讀者現在必須冷靜地接受這樣的想法：在這門心理學中，他所面對的是某種獨一無二的東西，若非如此，便是某種全然非理性、宗派主義或奧秘的智慧；否則，還有什麼能促使所有科學權威在門檻前便轉身離去，並徹底拒絕跨過它呢？
